metadata.title: Displacement Governance Mapping
metadata.positioning: A comparative diagnostic framework that maps governance fault lines, institutional absorption capacity, fiscal architecture, and reform pathways across displacement contexts.
metadata.note: This is not a displacement database. It is a governance and fiscal architecture diagnostic platform.

fault_lines.categorical_note: Inclusion is mediated by legal status, registration and administrative discretion.
fault_lines.temporal_note: Some support is embedded in ordinary systems, but displacement-specific obligations remain uneven.
fault_lines.territorial_note: Service access depends on registration, locality and program rules.

fault_lines.categorical_note: Refugee and stateless populations face strong status-based exclusion from ordinary systems.
fault_lines.temporal_note: Support is heavily project-based and externally mediated for non-citizen displaced groups.
fault_lines.territorial_note: Mobility and documentation status shape access across jurisdictions.

fault_lines.categorical_note: Citizens affected by disasters connect to national systems more readily than refugees or non-citizens.
fault_lines.temporal_note: Disaster systems provide continuity, but refugee inclusion remains limited.
fault_lines.territorial_note: Benefits and services are shaped by local implementation and registration.

fault_lines.categorical_note: Disaster and conflict displacement systems exist, but implementation varies by geography and status.
fault_lines.temporal_note: Emergency and recovery financing is active, but recurrent inclusion is uneven.
fault_lines.territorial_note: Portability improves through national systems but remains constrained by local delivery.

fault_lines.categorical_note: Conflict, citizenship exclusion and governance fragmentation create severe access barriers.
fault_lines.temporal_note: Support is heavily humanitarian and disrupted by conflict conditions.
fault_lines.territorial_note: Access changes sharply across areas of control, movement routes and documentation status.

fault_lines.categorical_note: Disaster-affected citizens are more connected to systems than informal migrants or non-citizens.
fault_lines.temporal_note: Climate and disaster systems create partial continuity but displacement is not consistently framed as recurrent.
fault_lines.territorial_note: Household registration and locality influence access and portability.

fault_lines.categorical_note: Expanding social systems still leave documentation and mobility-related gaps.
fault_lines.temporal_note: Displacement-related support remains strongly project and donor shaped.
fault_lines.territorial_note: Local capacity and documentation constraints limit portability.

fault_lines.categorical_note: System maturity, documentation and remoteness create strong access barriers.
fault_lines.temporal_note: Support is often project-linked rather than recurrently embedded.
fault_lines.territorial_note: Services and fiscal capacity are uneven across localities.
